title: "Screen only displays 640x480@60Hz"
date: 2007-11-06
forum: Absolute Beginner Talk
---

### Post by cf1709 on 2007-11-06
I was contented with my 1024x768 resolution until I restarted my pc. After which, the resolution has changed into 640x480@60Hz and I can't change it. What should I do? I have a NVIDIA 6200 video card and my monitor is Emachines eView 17s. Here's what is written in my xorg.conf

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Thu Oct  4 10:33:51 PDT 2007

# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"

	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen      0  "Default Screen" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "CorePointer"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
    Option         "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "stylus"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
    Option         "Type" "stylus"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "eraser"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
    Option         "Type" "eraser"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "cursor"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
    Option         "Type" "cursor"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "eView 17s"
    VendorName     "Plug 'n' Play"
    ModelName      "Plug 'n' Play"
    Gamma           1
    ModeLine       "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -hsync -vsync
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

 #    
    Identifier     "monitor1"
    VendorName     "Plug 'n' Play"
    ModelName      "Plug 'n' Play"
    Gamma           1
    ModeLine       "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -hsync -vsync
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Generic Video Card"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    BoardName      "VESA driver (generic)"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          0
EndSection

Section "Device"

 #    
    Identifier     "device1"
    Driver         "vesa"
    BoardName      "VESA driver (generic)"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "Generic Video Card"
    Monitor        "eView 17s"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Virtual     640 480
        Depth       24
        Modes      "640x480@60"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"

 #    
    Identifier     "screen1"
    Device         "device1"
    Monitor        "monitor1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "640x480@60"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection

```

---

### Post by rudeboyskunk on 2007-11-06
After all those sections that have "640x480@60", hit your spacebar and add "1024x768@xx" where xx is the refresh rate you want.  That should work.

---

### Post by cf1709 on 2007-11-06
I did what you said and restarted the PC. Now, Ubuntu only runs at low resolution mode (at least it went up to 800x600). Now here's what is written in the xorg.conf after the restart:

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Thu Oct  4 10:33:51 PDT 2007

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0"
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 110.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 150.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

I forgot to say that I'm a newbie to Ubuntu so I don't know much about this xorg.conf thing. Hope you can still help.

---

### Post by alienexplorers on 2007-11-06
Delete the modelines in your monitor sections.  Then run in terminal:
> gksudo nvidia-settings
Use this to set your resolution and refresh.

---

### Post by cf1709 on 2007-11-06
Sorry for being a n00b but, when you said "Delete the modelines in your monitor sections." What do you mean? Sorry again...:(

---

### Post by alienexplorers on 2007-11-06
Skip the modelines!!!  Just run from the terminal:
> gksudo nvidia-settings

---

### Post by cf1709 on 2007-11-06
I solved the problem with regards to the image above. But I got back to the 640x480 resolution. I ran 'gksudo nvidia-settings' but the windows are too big for me to see all those choices! I can't reach the bottom parts of the window. Hope somebody helps... :(

### Post by overdrank on 2007-11-06
> **cf1709 said:**
> I solved the problem with regards to the image above. But I got back to the 640x480 resolution. I ran 'gksudo nvidia-settings' but the windows are too big for me to see all those choices! I can't reach the bottom parts of the window. Hope somebody helps... :(

Try moving the windows by holding the alt key and single click and hold then move the window by moving the mouse.

---

### Post by cf1709 on 2007-11-07
Thanks for the help. I can now move the windows. However, the resolution choices are frustrating as 640x480 is the maximum resolution on the list.

### Post by poudin on 2007-11-07
try Envy pacakge

install it and it will add a menu to your Applications --> System Tools...then run it and it can auto detect your ATI or Nvidia Settings add make the appropriate xorg edits automatically..

i have a hp pavillion 9000 laptop with Widescreen and it set my 3D driver beautifully

---

### Post by jack0rlando on 2008-05-23
Hey i had exactly the same problem. Just start ubuntu in recovery mode(press escape on boot) and select reconfigure x-server. Hope that helps

---

### Post by IainPurdie on 2008-05-24
Hey folks - I'm having similar problems. I'm remote-administering a 6.06 LTS box via VNC. When the machine's rebooted with no monitor attached, it default to 640x480 and there's no option to select another resolution. That's what we're stuck with.

I can't access safe mode, as VNC will only work once the machine's up and logged in! I had a look in package manager for the ENvy package mentioned above, but the only one I could spot detected sound cards, not VGA ones.

Any help much appreciated! It's not a showstopper, but being on a "small" screen when remotely administrating is a pain!

---
